This chart provides some good context for where we are today. You can see a few anomalies on the chart. 

Two examples include 2018 and 2020. In 2018, failed expectations of material inventory draws in Q4 2018 led to the largest long liquidation in money manager positioning in history.

In 2020, massive demand destruction led to a supply glut that quickly translated to much lower oil prices down the road.

Today, the market is essentially pricing in builds to reverse from 1.05 billion bbls (expected) back to 1.2 billion bbls in the US.

This is my analysis of the Strait of Hormuz and you can see it in the video from @MarineTraffic. 

Tanker inflows remain heavily restricted. I have included a table of June 24 data thus far.

What's obvious to me is that there's an exodus of tankers using the Oman route. You can see this at the beginning of the video and towards the end of the video. There's another group ready to leave.

The US appears to be coordinating with these groups to support transit. 

Again, most of the traffic is outbound as they are trying to get stranded tankers out.

IRGC just announced that tankers must adhere to the rules. But so far, there's not much enforcement on the Oman lane. The longer the transits continue on the Oman lane, the faster IRGC will lose control of the Strait. This means that escalation is a high probability scenario.

tweets.text:
Paul Tudor Jones on what he learned from Warren Buffet:

 "I used to teach a class and my big lesson is you're going to make your money by riding a trend for the longest time.   

I used to sit there and rail on Warren Buffett year after year. 

I'd say he just happened to be in the right place at the right time and caught this bull market.  

Our fund has a minus 0.12 correlation with the S&P 500 over 40 years. So 100% of our returns are alpha. 

That's the difference between investing and trading.  

I was just thinking, why couldn't I be Warren Buffett? Just believe in America, and when you're down 50%, who cares, because America's gonna bring you through.

I feel like I've been a right guard in the NFL for 50 years, fighting in the trenches every day.  

I've always envied that belief system. It's worked so well for so long. 

He understood the power of compound interest at nine years old. What a genius."

My guest today is Paul Tudor Jones (@ptj_official), one of the greatest macro traders of all time.

BREAKING: The Fed’s balance sheet rose +$11 billion in the week ending June 17th, to $6.74 trillion, the highest since March 2025.

Total assets have risen +$162.8 billion since the start of the year.

This has been driven by Treasury holdings, which have surged +$251.8 billion over this period, to $4.49 trillion, the highest since June 2024.

At the same time, holdings of Mortgage-Backed Securities have declined -$74.2 billion, to $1.96 trillion, the lowest since September 2020.

The Fed's balance sheet is now 76% above pre-pandemic levels.

The Fed's balance sheet continues to expand.

tweets.text:
The US wealth divide has never been bigger:

The top 20% of earners now account for ~58% of all personal spending in the US, the highest proportion on record. 

At the same time, the bottom 80% account for just ~42%, the lowest on record.

This comes as personal outlays growth for the top 20% has averaged +8.3% per year since the 2020 pandemic, almost double the +4.5% seen for the bottom 80%.

Over the last year, the gap has widened further, with the top 20% seeing a +6.5% increase in spending, compared to +2.6% for the bottom 80%.

To put this into perspective, in the 1990s, both groups accounted for roughly equal proportions of total personal spending, at ~50% each.

Asset owners are the only winners in this economy.

tweets.text:
Everyone is piling into US stocks.

Foreign holdings of US equities jumped +$2.0 trillion in April, to a record $23.2 trillion.

This figure has more than DOUBLED since the 2022 bear market.

By comparison, during the 2020 pandemic, non-US investors held $7.5 trillion of US equities, or 68% less.

Foreign investors now own ~30.5% of the $76 trillion US equity market.

Furthermore, foreign investors’ allocation to US equities as a percentage of US financial assets is up to 63%, an all-time high and 10 percentage points above the 2000 Dot-Com Bubble peak.

Foreign investors are all-in on US stocks.

Mirendil Co-Founder and CEO Behnam Neyshabur explains the path to self-accelerating AI:

" When you think about what does a scientist or an engineer do, the main skill they have is get very deep in a domain... and build a very sharp expertise that accumulates over time."

"As you kind of become expert in a domain, your expertise is becoming sharper and sharper in something that has a tiny bit of volume in the whole set of all possible things you could be working on."

"And getting to that point, being able to get there is a capability that is needed to advance all areas of science."

"What that view means is that you have to work on what does it take to direct a system toward like a particular angle and make fast improvement, and that's the self-accelerating AI technology."

@bneyshabur @MirendilAI

We're thrilled to lead Mirendil's $200M seed round.

Frontier AI work has been locked inside a few big labs.

@MirendilAI is building a system that can help anyone do AI work: they train frontier models that are expert at AI R&D and build the product around it. The result is a system that loops over research and engineering problems on its own, making progress without human intervention.

It's like a coding agent built for AI research that controls its own GPUs.

Mirendil is one of the few teams with the experience and the priors to make the end-to-end system work. @bneyshabur, @HarshMeh1a, @shayan_, and @tararezaeikh come out of Anthropic, Google DeepMind, and xAI.

Welcome to the a16z family, Mirendil.

I have a thesis that the OG Web3 is dead.
Everything is moving to Web2.5.

@chainlink case is one of the many – look what happened ↓

Chainlink Labs joined Project Pangea, a coalition of 47 banks targeting real-time stablecoin-based FX settlement across the Europe–South Korea trade corridor.

The project aims to replace T+2 with near-instant T+0 atomic PvP settlement using regulated euro- and won-pegged stablecoins.

This is a meaningful institutional adoption signal for Chainlink’s oracle and cross-chain infrastructure.

Chainlink will become the boring infra for classic finance.

Is it good? Yeah.
Is it changing crypto as we know it?
Also yeah.

Finally recovered from a few days of illness hence the lack of much posting on here

Back at the desk properly today — there is so much happening under the hood post-FOMC… imo you want to be looking closely at 30y real rates and whether they break new YTD lows (as I expect they will) 

We have had 4+ years of 30y real rates rising — ROC is now turning negative and it appears there is a ceiling at ~2.9%

The recent move in DXY largely reflective of the flattening in the curve — whilst potentially a headwind further down the line (depending on how far DXY pushes higher but more importantly how long it stays bid), I think the picture for the next 6 months remains very supportive 

Will have more detailed thoughts on Monday and will share some more stuff later today but exciting times ahead imo

Look at the comments under this and ask yourself why it only now feels so comfortable to be bearish; why it feels like much lower is guaranteed and therefore why even bother being an ‘early’ buyer at 60? 

Everything has been perfectly aligned for lower prices: 4y cycle + summer + Strategy fud + perceived quantum risks — and yet: since putting in the February low at 60, the market has failed to break meaningfully lower at every opportunity 

And each day that passes by *without* lower prices, the chorus grows louder that lower prices are coming and are inevitable 

Being contrarian for its own sake is nonsense — but when the R/R profile of the setup is this skewed (sentiment+narrative utterly convinced of an opportunity to buy much lower in 3-6 months but data already suggestive of historical extremes), it’s hard not to look at that and think of ‘being greedy when others are fearful’

Perhaps we do get much lower; or perhaps we don’t get another bull market in Bitcoin for years to come — but I am seeing a golden opportunity here that is being met with complete apathy and disinterest

🇨🇳 Direct financing through bonds and equity in #China is increasingly overtaking bank lending as an important source of new funding, marking a structural shift in the financial system, the Shanghai Securities News says in a report.

➡ China’s shift toward direct financing may have reached a tipping point, says Lian Ping, chairman of the China Chief Economists Forum, with bonds and equity beginning to outpace loans in new funding since 2025.

➡ Lian said the trend reflects an economic transition toward technology and emerging industries, which in the early days rely more on capital markets, venture funding and bond issuance than traditional bank credit.

➡Ming Ming, chief economist at Citic Securities, expects direct financing to account for more than 50% of total social financing within the next five to 10 years.

*Link (Chinese): https://t.co/XgGcMGD6GL

🇺🇸 The US bank stress test results did not get a huge amount of attention but it signals that the US banking system is not the weak link in the cycle today.

📊 The Fed tested 32 large banks under a severe recession scenario, with unemployment rising to 10%, a sharp correction in both residential and commercial real estate, and significant credit stress. Despite more than $700bn in hypothetical losses, the aggregate capital ratio would only fall from 12.8% to 11.2%.

✅ This is clearly bullish for financials but also positive for the broader US market because a solid banking system reduces the risk of a credit crunch. If banks are well capitalized, they can continue financing the economy instead of sharply cutting credit to households and businesses. This supports risk-on sentiment and reduces the risk of a systemic accident.

🏦 An other important point is that banks can also restart capital returns to shareholders. Several major institutions have already announced dividend increases or share buyback programs. Therefore, banks are not only just surviving the test but they have enough excess capital to reward shareholders.

The United States remains firmly in a position of economic and financial resilience.

*Fed link: https://t.co/7thuva3KJa

tweets.text:
Very interesting statement today: $MU CEO predicts a multi-decade memory demand cycle driven by humanoid robots.

"Humanoid robots, he says, will require roughly ten times more memory than today’s Level 2+ autonomous vehicles."

"And that demand wave is set to begin before the decade is out."

Something as well as was "Over time, we expect the value of on-device AI combined with pent-up unit replacement demand to drive memory demand growth"

Which is also another trend (Apple Intelligence is currently dog, but I'm sure we'll see innovations with localized/edge AI). 

Feels like all the industry leaders from $TSM Chairman, $TSLA Elon Musk, to $MU CEO see humanoids as the next major trend so physical AI is probably next. 

I wonder if the world is going to have enough memory. Or if we'll see enough breakthroughs to shrink memory usage.

Think so. But in the meantime, I call my strategy: Diversified Losses.

With $AXTI, $SOI, $AAOI, and many others.

Had a massive drawdown recently, 
CPO exposure was hit the hardest (Foci, Msscorp, etc) and adjacent names, feels bad.

Probably lesson personally, I had too much concentration in photonics vs. memory/other sectors without weighting/hedging properly.

With Soitec, there's been a few negative institutional reports that I'd disagree with. 

Think AXT was hit harder in specific just because of float expansion/dilution concerns. AAOI, probably just brought down with the theme.

I can't give advice on buying, so completely up to you to make for cost averaging or entering positions.

But I do think we're still early in the Supercycle with photonics, there's bound to be corrections/crashes along the way up.

If my personal thesis is correct though, many of these names will have a major inflection point in midway through 2027 scaling up to 2028.

Markets don't typically wait to price things in advance, but some ideas might be a tad early or in the buildout given it's H2 2026 now.

Which is why it's important to build your own conviction.

tweets.text:
on the state of "digital credit"-

there has been a lot of noise around what is going on in the preferreds market, good theories, bad explanations, almost all certainly with uncertain proofs. at times like this when the future looks inscrutable, its important to reduce all abstractions into the most knowable facts to be prepared for the future. here are three bullets that id like to share in that spirit:  

1) mNAV and BPS have been intellectually inconsistent since i called it out now over a year ago. this particular reckoning is at some level a reflection of the pent up mismatch between expectation and reality. the industry needs to do better and im optimistic that after mr. market's voting machine the healing process can finally begin

2) despite its colloquial characterization, preferred equities are definitionally not credit in the sense of its legal protection afforded to debtors, and is absolutely not a money market fund replacement. analogies can sometimes be useful to draw heuristics but you obfuscate the core differences at your own peril

3) nonetheless, digital credit has found remarkable product market fit. over 10bn in monthly trading volume across only a few tickers is heroic for a new product category in finance, and remains one of the most liquid securities in our capital markets today. in fact,  it is singlehandedly the largest preferreds segment that has ever existed in US history and the largest issuance was the largest IPO in 2025. i expect these instruments to be issued by many strategically important companies going forward to raise flexible funds for strategic assets/needs

always remember that cryptos core mission is to fix the unfair manipulation of time by technocrats, to fix the physics of money by reunderwriting the concept of duration beyond the risk free rate by introducing continuous spreads in the most dynamic mechanism possible with the most amount of decentralized liquidity the world has ever known. thats why perpetual futures is the biggest TAM the global capital markets has ever seen in modern history, as is prediction markets, as will be perpetual prefs.

while some may be in despair with the current price action, i believe in hindsight this will be remembered as a historic moment to upgrade the industry, its leaders, its products, and most importantly its customers. you will always be served well if you just remember that every bet in crypto is ultimately an expression of two ideologies: volatility is vitality, and liquidity is liberty.

now go for a stretch, and strive to always be learning
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
BPS and mNAV when taken together are intellectually inconsistent-

BPS is per Share, so does not incorporate leverage (but should)

And mNAV, despite the name (NET ASSET VALUE, which excludes liabilities) is using EV (which traditionally INCLUDES debt)

These details matter

Firms: CryptoForensic Investigators or ZeroShadow 

Analytics companies: Chainalysis or TRM

Everyone else has a lower quality threshold for output or is incapable.

Slow Mist & Elliptic can be ok just a bit less consistent. 

Good onchain research is still rare due to a lack of overall talent and understanding.

tweets.text:
a book everyone excited about the agentic economy should read - Accelerando by Charles Stross

the main character has a metacortex - a distributed cloud of software agents surrounding him in "netspace," borrowing CPU cycles from nearby processors and spawning new sub‑agents to research opportunities while he moves through the world

these agents continuously negotiate over compute, bandwidth, and informational “payoff,” treating economics as a game where abundance has replaced scarcity

markets and games blur together—scarcity is an old "primate superstition", and the real contest is how elegantly you can route compute toward interesting problems

i continue to believe reading sci fi is the highest ROI activity for anyone who wants to build for the future

every single time i think the ai memory investment is over i'm proven wrong, micron earnings is yet another sign the cyclical nature of memory has broken

-> 50%+ of micron's revenue comes from 16 strategic customer deals. thats $100B minimum over the next few years from 14 customers.

-> these customers are signing 5-year terms upfront. the norm is 1-2 years. guaranteed payment whether they need the memory supply or not.

anthropic just signed an exclusive partnership with micron yday. apple, google and nvidia are trying their hardest to secure more supply from SK and samsung

memory demand is increasingly faster than models are being optimized to use less memory. the direction for more memory scarcity is getting hard to ignore tbh
